title: Complexity Baseline and Budget
outOfScope: Changing product source or project-specific quality commands
Activating a new complexity budget without an explicit policy record
Treating unavailable baseline evidence as an allow decision or as proof of readiness

sources.reason: WI07 establishes Adoption, Active, and Work Item complexity baselines.
Repository Truth, Evidence Governance, and Work Item terminology.
Current complexity measurements, deltas, and repayment enforcement.
Existing provenance-bound baseline capture and comparison helpers.
Complexity policy and report behavior tests.

riskAssessment.reason: Baseline provenance can be incomplete in an adopter environment; unavailable evidence must remain explicit and non-allowing.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized the serial remediation plan and complete Work Item lifecycle.

preReviewWarnings: Baseline identities and unavailable states must remain visible; budget increases require repayment ownership and due dates.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Complexity evidence records distinct Adoption, Active, and Work Item base commits with source identity and an explicit unavailable state when a commit cannot be resolved.

The complexity report compares current measurements against the selected baseline and classifies historical debt, Work Item-added change, and regression/deterioration without copying template limits as adopter policy.
A budget increase is accepted only with a repayment record containing metric, previous/current values, owner, due date, and remediation rationale.
Focused tests cover bound baseline selection, missing/unavailable evidence, historical versus Work Item deltas, regression detection, and repayment enforcement.
English and Japanese adoption documentation explains baseline provenance, unavailable evidence, and the human review boundary.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The template currently measures repository complexity and compares it to a mutable policy baseline, but an adopter needs provenance-bound Adoption, Active, and Work Item baselines to distinguish inherited history from changes introduced by the current Work Item and from deterioration.
constraints: Use existing standard-library baseline evidence and Git identity helpers; do not infer an unavailable commit.
Keep historical debt, Work Item delta, and regression/deterioration as separate signals.
Do not copy template maximums into adopter policy or silently convert unavailable evidence into allow.
Budget increases must retain owner, due date, previous/current values, and repayment rationale.
Do not claim complexity evidence is a security, compliance, or production-readiness proof.
intent.rationale: Explicit baseline provenance makes complexity changes reviewable and prevents inherited template debt from being misattributed to the current Work Item.

rawUserRequest: 先走完当前文档的流程。用户已授权按计划逐工单执行，包含修改代码、测试、文档、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理；妄想测试失败继续修改。

declaredIntent.summary: Establish provenance-bound complexity baselines and separate historical, Work Item, and regression signals.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: three baseline identities bind to explicit commits or report unavailable
historical debt is distinct from Work Item-added complexity
regression/deterioration and budget increases fail closed without repayment evidence

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized governance code, tests, documentation, PR, merge, archive, close, and branch cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
